A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a high-impact scholarship application due Thursday, which requires focused effort rather than immediate fun.
Starting the scholarship application now builds a buffer against potential delays, reducing future stress without sacrificing any future leisure options.
After an hour of work, the scholarship application is still only 32% complete with three days remaining. I need to maintain this momentum to finish before the deadline.
I made solid progress on the scholarship application, moving from 32% to 49% complete with three days left. I missed a casual gaming opportunity but prioritized the urgent deadline.
After one hour of work, the scholarship application is now 55% complete with three days remaining. I have made steady progress but still need to maintain focus to finish the required tasks.
After two hours of work, the scholarship application is 64% complete with three days left. I am on track but still need to finish the remaining short essay and uploads.
After one more hour, the scholarship application is 68% complete with three days remaining; I am making steady progress but still need to finish the essay and uploads.
After two hours of work, the scholarship application is 76% complete with three days left; I am making steady progress but still need to finish the essay and uploads.
After sleeping 8 hours, my energy is fully restored to 9.6 with no sleep debt, putting me in an ideal state to finish the remaining scholarship application tasks.
The scholarship application is now fully complete with the essay written and uploads finished. I can relax knowing this urgent task is done three days early.
The scholarship application is now fully complete after one hour of work, leaving three days of buffer before the deadline.
The scholarship application is confirmed complete with no further work needed, leaving me three days early and stress-free.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
